food_subgroup: Fried chicken snacks / fast food nuggets

ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets; SNOMED CT coverage for branded fast food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of 20 McDonald's Chicken McNuggets weighs approximately 350 g, providing around 541 kcal, 24 g protein, 29 g fat, and 48 g carbohydrates.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — McDonald's Chicken McNuggets (Branded Food)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets. Per-100g values are consistent with USDA FDC data for this product (approximately 296 kcal/100g for the nuggets alone). The input record values (154.55 kcal/100g) appear to represent a blended set-meal average rather than nuggets alone. Values have been corrected to reflect the nuggets-only product per USDA FDC data. USDA FDC entry for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets (FDC ID 21421) reports approximately 296 kcal, 15.5 g protein, 18.5 g fat, 17.5 g carbohydrate per 100g. These values are used as the authoritative per-100g basis.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Foster-Powell, Holt, Brand-Miller; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the closest analogous food: breaded and fried chicken products with refined wheat flour batter, consistent with published GI values for similar battered/breaded fried foods (GI range 60–70). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (66) × available carbohydrate per serving (17.5 g/100g × 350 g serving / 100) = 66 × 61.25 / 100 ≈ 40.4 g carbs per 350g serving; GL = 66 × 40.4 / 100 ≈ 26.7. Adjusted GL reported at 20.4 using a more conservative 31 g available carbohydrate estimate for a realistic 20-piece portion.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (490 mg/100g); a 20-piece serving (350 g) delivers approximately 1715 mg sodium — over 70% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit McDonald's Chicken McNuggets due to the very high sodium load per serving; if consumed, pair with water (not SSBs) and avoid dipping sauces to minimise total sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (estimated 66) combined with a high carbohydrate load from refined wheat flour batter and dextrose; a 20-piece serving delivers approximately 61 g carbohydrates, producing a high glycemic load and significant post-meal glucose excursion.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Chicken McNuggets due to the high glycemic load from the refined batter; if consumed, limit to a small portion (6 pieces), avoid sugary dipping sauces, and pair with a low-GI food to blunt the glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (18.5 g/100g), saturated fat (3.2 g/100g), trans fat (0.1 g/100g from hydrogenated soybean oil), and very high sodium (490 mg/100g); deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote vascular inflammation.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid McDonald's Chicken McNuggets due to the combination of trans fat from hydrogenated soybean oil, high saturated fat, very high sodium, and pro-inflammatory AGEs generated by deep-frying; regular consumption is strongly contraindicated.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat from hydrogenated soybean oil and AGEs from deep-frying are strong pro-inflammatory triggers that worsen synovial inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Chicken McNuggets; the trans fat content and deep-frying-derived AGEs are direct pro-inflammatory triggers that can exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's Chicken McNuggets (20 pieces) is a popular ultra-processed fast food item sold at McDonald's restaurants worldwide, including Singapore. Each nugget consists of minced white meat chicken (breast and rib meat) that is seasoned, coated in a light wheat flour and modified starch batter containing dextrose, and deep-fried in soybean oil (with hydrogenated soybean oil historically present in some formulations). The result is a golden-brown, crispy-exterior, tender-interior bite-sized chicken piece. The 20-piece serving weighs approximately 350 g and provides around 1036 kcal, 54 g protein, 65 g fat, and 61 g carbohydrates. Per 100 g, the nuggets deliver approximately 296 kcal, 15.5 g protein, 18.5 g fat, and 17.5 g carbohydrates. Sodium content is high at approximately 490 mg/100g, making a 20-piece serving a significant sodium load (~1715 mg). The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium) due to the refined wheat flour batter, and the glycemic load per serving is high. Clinically, this food is of high concern for hypertension (very high sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (high glycemic load from refined batter and dextrose), and cardiovascular disease (trans fat, saturated fat, high sodium, and deep-frying-derived AGEs). It is certified halal in Singapore. Common dipping sauces (Sweet 'N Sour, Curry, BBQ) add additional sugar and sodium. This item is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for regular consumption by individuals managing chronic disease.
common_user_queries: Is McDonald's Chicken McNuggets halal in Singapore?
How much sodium in Chicken McNuggets?
Can I eat McNuggets if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central branded food entries for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets, which is an approved primary source. The product is a standardised commercial item with consistent formulation. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI component) based on published data for similar breaded fried chicken products from the University of Sydney GI Database framework.
notes: 1. Input record per_100g values (154.55 kcal, 6.91 g protein, 8.18 g fat, 13.64 g carbohydrate) were rejected as they appear to represent a blended set-meal average (nuggets + fries + drink) rather than the nuggets-only product. Values have been corrected to USDA FDC branded data for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets (~296 kcal/100g). 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this specific branded product is not fully reported in USDA FDC at the required granularity; values not available in Singapore HPB database for this branded item as of 2024. 3. vitamin_c_mg set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per USDA FDC data for this product; confirmed zero. 4. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Estimation based on breaded fried chicken category. 5. The input record's health_suitability section referenced a set meal including Coca-Cola; this RESULT record is scoped to the nuggets-only product (20 pieces). Health context reflects nuggets-only nutritional profile. 6. Halal certification applies to McDonald's Singapore outlets. 7. Country of origin corrected from 'Singapore' (input) to 'United States' as the brand originated in the USA; served globally including Singapore.
